sections.title: The "Eyes" (OCR, Layout & Vision)
sections.goal: Convert pixels into trustworthy text with spatial proof (bounding boxes).

sections.title: The "Biologists" (NER, Ontology & Linking)
sections.goal: Understand that "brca1" is a gene and map it to a global ID.

sections.title: The "Brain" (Retrieval, Storage & Analytics)
sections.goal: Store data efficiently and identify connections.

sections.title: The "Lab Manager" (Ops, Stats & Eval)
sections.goal: Keep the pipeline running and prove that it works.

libraries.name: Surya OCR
libraries.github_description: OCR, layout analysis, reading order, table recognition in 90+ languages
libraries.pypi_summary: OCR, layout, reading order, and table recognition in 90+ languages
libraries.pro: Precision & Speed. Detects line-level bounding boxes in one GPU pass.
libraries.con: Resource Heavy. Requires 16GB VRAM for efficiency.

libraries.name: Marker
libraries.github_description: Convert PDF to markdown + JSON quickly with high accuracy
libraries.pypi_summary: Convert documents to markdown with high speed and accuracy.
libraries.pro: Math & Speed. Best-in-class at cleaning messy equations/headers into Markdown.
libraries.con: Heuristic Limits. Struggles with complex table structures.

libraries.name: Docling
libraries.github_description: Get your documents ready for gen AI
libraries.pypi_summary: SDK and CLI for parsing PDF, DOCX, HTML, and more, to a unified document representation for powering downstream workflows such as gen AI applications.
libraries.pro: The "Second Opinion". A completely independent parsing engine (IBM).
libraries.con: Complexity. Complex setup adds a heavy dependency chain.

libraries.name: PP-StructureV3
libraries.github_description: Turn any PDF or image document into structured data for your AI. A powerful, lightweight OCR toolkit that bridges the gap between images/PDFs and LLMs. Supports 100+ languages.
libraries.pypi_summary: Awesome multilingual OCR and document parsing toolkits based on PaddlePaddle
libraries.pro: Multilingual Robustness. Excellent at borderless tables.
libraries.con: Ecosystem Lock. Uses PaddlePaddle (not PyTorch), requiring a separate container.

libraries.name: Nougat
libraries.github_description: Implementation of Nougat Neural Optical Understanding for Academic Documents
libraries.pypi_summary: Nougat: Neural Optical Understanding for Academic Documents
libraries.pro: End-to-End. Good for pure math papers where layout doesn't matter.
libraries.con: Hallucination Risk. Generates text without bounding boxes (unverifiable).

libraries.name: Donut
libraries.github_description: Official Implementation of OCR-free Document Understanding Transformer (Donut) and Synthetic Document Generator (SynthDoG), ECCV 2022
libraries.pro: OCR-Free. Skips text recognition, goes straight to JSON.
libraries.con: Fragile. Requires massive fine-tuning for each new document type.

libraries.name: pytesseract
libraries.github_description: A Python wrapper for Google Tesseract
libraries.pypi_summary: Python-tesseract is a python wrapper for Google's Tesseract-OCR
libraries.pro: Reliability. Used only to check page rotation before heavy models run.
libraries.con: Accuracy. Too inaccurate for final data extraction.

libraries.name: Florence-2
libraries.pro: Semantic Vision. Can "look" at a page and find "Figure 1" without needing text.
libraries.con: Generative Risk. It can hallucinate if not strictly prompted.

libraries.name: Qwen2.5-VL
libraries.github_description: Qwen3-VL is the multimodal large language model series developed by Qwen team, Alibaba Cloud.
libraries.pro: The Tie-Breaker. If Surya and Marker disagree, this model "looks" at the crop to decide.
libraries.con: Expensive. Very slow; only used on the <5% of data where conflicts exist.

libraries.name: RT-DETR
libraries.github_description: [CVPR 2024] Official RT-DETR (RTDETR paddle pytorch), Real-Time DEtection TRansformer, DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection. 🔥 🔥 🔥
libraries.pro: Real-Time Speed. Identifies headers/footers instantly.
libraries.con: Thresholding. Needs tuning to distinguish "Caption" from "Text".

libraries.name: LayoutLMv3
libraries.github_description: Large-scale Self-supervised Pre-training Across Tasks, Languages, and Modalities
libraries.pro: Standard. The academic baseline for classifying "Title" vs "Paragraph".
libraries.con: Token Limit. Can't read a whole dense page (512 tokens max).

libraries.name: DiT
libraries.github_description: Large-scale Self-supervised Pre-training Across Tasks, Languages, and Modalities
libraries.pro: Vision Only. Classification without needing OCR text first.
libraries.con: Blind spots. Can't read the text content to help classification.

libraries.name: PyMuPDF
libraries.github_description: PyMuPDF is a high performance Python library for data extraction, analysis, conversion & manipulation of PDF (and other) documents.
libraries.pypi_summary: A high performance Python library for data extraction, analysis, conversion & manipulation of PDF (and other) documents.
libraries.pro: Speed. The fastest way to turn PDFs into images for the vision models.
libraries.con: License. AGPL (strict), but acceptable for academic research.

libraries.name: pdfplumber
libraries.github_description: Plumb a PDF for detailed information about each char, rectangle, line, et cetera — and easily extract text and tables.
libraries.pypi_summary: Plumb a PDF for detailed information about each char, rectangle, and line.
libraries.pro: Debuggability. Great for visualizing what the computer "sees".
libraries.con: Sluggish. Too slow for processing 100k+ pages.

libraries.name: pdf2image
libraries.github_description: A python module that wraps the pdftoppm utility to convert PDF to PIL Image object
libraries.pypi_summary: A wrapper around the pdftoppm and pdftocairo command line tools to convert PDF to a PIL Image list.
libraries.pro: Simplicity. Just wraps Poppler. Rock solid.
libraries.con: System Dep. Requires installing Poppler on the OS level.

libraries.name: PyMuPDF
libraries.github_description: PyMuPDF is a high performance Python library for data extraction, analysis, conversion & manipulation of PDF (and other) documents.
libraries.pypi_summary: A high performance Python library for data extraction, analysis, conversion & manipulation of PDF (and other) documents.
libraries.pro: Structure Preservation. Rotates pages without breaking the text layer.
libraries.con: Ambiguity. Sometimes metadata rotation conflicts with visual rotation.

libraries.name: MinerU
libraries.github_description: Transforms complex documents like PDFs into LLM-ready markdown/JSON for your Agentic workflows.
libraries.pypi_summary: A practical tool for converting PDF to Markdown
libraries.pro: Comprehensive. Full-pipeline document parsing with layout analysis.
libraries.con: New. Less battle-tested than Marker or Docling.

libraries.name: LayoutReader
libraries.github_description: Large-scale Self-supervised Pre-training Across Tasks, Languages, and Modalities
libraries.pro: Reading Order. Determines correct reading order from layout.
libraries.con: Integration. Lives inside the unilm monorepo.

libraries.name: LayoutReader
libraries.github_description: Large-scale Self-supervised Pre-training Across Tasks, Languages, and Modalities
libraries.pro: Reading Order. Determines correct reading order from layout.
libraries.con: Integration. Lives inside the unilm monorepo.

libraries.name: TATR-Detect
libraries.github_description: Table Transformer (TATR) is a deep learning model for extracting tables from unstructured documents (PDFs and images). This is also the official repository for the PubTables-1M dataset and GriTS evaluation metric.
libraries.pro: Scientific Bias. Trained on PubTables-1M, specifically for academic journals.
libraries.con: Cropping. Requires perfect image crops; if we clip the edge, detection fails.

libraries.name: TATR-Struct
libraries.github_description: Table Transformer (TATR) is a deep learning model for extracting tables from unstructured documents (PDFs and images). This is also the official repository for the PubTables-1M dataset and GriTS evaluation metric.
libraries.pro: Granularity. Recognizes row spans and column spans (complex headers).
libraries.con: Textless. Returns boxes only; we must "inject" text back in from Surya.

libraries.name: TableFormer
libraries.pypi_summary: This package contains the AI models used by the Docling PDF conversion package
libraries.pro: Drift Resistance. Better at handling columns that "wave" or drift in scans.
libraries.con: Integrated. Hard to use outside of the Docling pipeline.

libraries.name: GROBID
libraries.github_description: A machine learning software for extracting information from scholarly documents
libraries.pro: The Specialist. Unbeaten precision for extracting Titles, DOIs, and Authors.
libraries.con: Rigid. If the paper is a non-conventional preprint, it might fail silently.

libraries.name: CERMINE
libraries.github_description: Content ExtRactor and MINEr
libraries.pro: Backup. Good if GROBID fails on a specific layout.
libraries.con: Java. Heavy memory usage (JVM).

libraries.name: Pydantic
libraries.github_description: Data validation using Python type hints
libraries.pypi_summary: Data validation using Python type hints
libraries.pro: Contract Enforcement. Ensures data fits the schema before it enters the DB.
libraries.con: Strictness. Can cause pipelines to crash on minor whitespace errors.

libraries.name: Pandera
libraries.github_description: A light-weight, flexible, and expressive statistical data testing library
libraries.pypi_summary: A light-weight and flexible data validation and testing tool for statistical data objects.
libraries.pro: Statistical Check. Checks values (e.g., "p-value must be < 1"), not just types.
libraries.con: Overhead. Slower on large DataFrames.

libraries.name: Marshmallow
libraries.github_description: A lightweight library for converting complex objects to and from simple Python datatypes.
libraries.pypi_summary: A lightweight library for converting complex datatypes to and from native Python datatypes.
libraries.pro: Legacy. Good for simple serialization.
libraries.con: Verbose. Requires more code than Pydantic.

libraries.name: RapidFuzz
libraries.github_description: Rapid fuzzy string matching in Python using various string metrics
libraries.pypi_summary: rapid fuzzy string matching
libraries.pro: Verification. Uses Levenshtein distance to compare Surya vs Marker text.
libraries.con: Dumb. Doesn't understand synonyms, only spelling.

libraries.name: FuzzyWuzzy
libraries.github_description: Fuzzy String Matching in Python
libraries.pypi_summary: Fuzzy string matching in python
libraries.pro: Simple. Easy API.
libraries.con: Slow. Pure Python (slow) vs RapidFuzz (C++).

libraries.name: BERN2
libraries.github_description: BERN2: an advanced neural biomedical namedentity recognition and normalization tool
libraries.pro: Breadth. Recognizes 9 types (Gene, Drug, Disease) simultaneously.
libraries.con: Rate Limits. The public API is throttled; local setup is hard.

libraries.name: HunFlair2
libraries.github_description: A very simple framework for state-of-the-art Natural Language Processing (NLP)
libraries.pypi_summary: A very simple framework for state-of-the-art NLP
libraries.pro: Consensus. Uses a different architecture (Flair); great for voting against BERN2.
libraries.con: Speed. Slower than transformer-only models.

libraries.name: MedCAT
libraries.github_description: Medical Concept Annotation Tool
libraries.pypi_summary: Medical Concept Annotation Toolkit (v2)
libraries.pro: Hospital Grade. The SOTA for mapping text to SNOMED-CT/UMLS.
libraries.con: Learning Curve. Requires training on the specific "Meta-Annotation" concept.

libraries.name: PubMedBERT
libraries.pro: Vocabulary. Trained only on medical text (no Wikipedia), so it understands jargon.
libraries.con: Assembly. Requires fine-tuning to actually do NER.

libraries.name: BioBERT
libraries.github_description: Bioinformatics'2020: BioBERT: a pre-trained biomedical language representation model for biomedical text mining
libraries.pro: Baseline. The standard comparison point.
libraries.con: Outdated. Outperformed by PubMedBERT in 2024 benchmarks.

libraries.name: PubTator
libraries.pro: Gold Standard. Pre-annotated by NCBI.
libraries.con: Coverage. Only covers papers already in PubMed.

libraries.name: GLiNER
libraries.github_description: Generalist and Lightweight Model for Named Entity Recognition (Extract any entity types from texts)
libraries.pypi_summary: Generalist model for NER (Extract any entity types from texts)
libraries.pro: Flexibility. Can find new entities (e.g., "Cohort Size") without retraining.
libraries.con: Newness. Less battle-tested than BERT.

libraries.name: scispaCy
libraries.github_description: A full spaCy pipeline and models for scientific/biomedical documents.
libraries.pypi_summary: A full SpaCy pipeline and models for scientific/biomedical documents.
libraries.pro: Preprocessing. Fast enough to run on millions of sentences for filtering.
libraries.con: Recall. Misses complex entities that BERN2 catches.

libraries.name: DOID
libraries.github_description: Repository for the Human Disease Ontology.
libraries.pro: Hierarchy. Good for high-level classification (Cancer vs Viral).
libraries.con: Narrow. Less granular than MONDO.

libraries.name: MONDO
libraries.github_description: Mondo Disease Ontology
libraries.pro: Unification. The "Rosetta Stone" connecting OMIM, DOID, and ORPHA IDs.
libraries.con: Flux. IDs can change between versions.

libraries.name: HPO
libraries.github_description: Ontology for the description of human clinical features
libraries.pro: Symptoms. The standard for "Human Phenotypes" (e.g., HP:000123).
libraries.con: Incomplete. Not every disease has a full HPO mapping.

libraries.name: SNOMED-CT
libraries.pro: Clinical. Essential if working with hospital records (EHR).
libraries.con: License. Requires a license for use.

libraries.name: spaCy
libraries.github_description: 💫 Industrial-strength Natural Language Processing (NLP) in Python
libraries.pypi_summary: Industrial-strength Natural Language Processing (NLP) in Python
libraries.pro: Engineering. The "Chassis" that holds the NER models together.
libraries.con: Generic. Needs plugins (scispaCy) for bio work.

libraries.name: Flair
libraries.github_description: A very simple framework for state-of-the-art Natural Language Processing (NLP)
libraries.pypi_summary: A very simple framework for state-of-the-art NLP
libraries.pro: State of Art. Research-grade sequence labeling.
libraries.con: Academic. Less "production ready" tooling than spaCy.

libraries.name: ColBERTv2
libraries.github_description: ColBERT: state-of-the-art neural search (SIGIR'20, TACL'21, NeurIPS'21, NAACL'22, CIKM'22, ACL'23, EMNLP'23)
libraries.pypi_summary: Efficient and Effective Passage Search via Contextualized Late Interaction over BERT
libraries.pro: Precision. Uses "Late Interaction" to match exact numbers/units.
libraries.con: Storage. Index is 10x larger than standard vectors.

libraries.name: Elasticsearch
libraries.github_description: Free and Open Source, Distributed, RESTful Search Engine
libraries.pypi_summary: Python client for Elasticsearch
libraries.pro: Features. The most robust engine for full-text search (BM25).
libraries.con: Maintenance. Heavy Java process; overkill for small data.

libraries.name: FAISS
libraries.github_description: A library for efficient similarity search and clustering of dense vectors.
libraries.pypi_summary: A library for efficient similarity search and clustering of dense vectors.
libraries.pro: Scale. Can search 1 Billion vectors in milliseconds.
libraries.con: Complexity. Low-level C++; hard to debug.

libraries.name: OpenSearch
libraries.github_description: 🔎 Open source distributed and RESTful search engine.
libraries.pypi_summary: Python client for OpenSearch
libraries.pro: Open Source. A truly open fork of Elasticsearch.
libraries.con: Lag. Features lag behind the proprietary Elastic.

libraries.name: Typesense
libraries.github_description: Open Source alternative to Algolia + Pinecone and an Easier-to-Use alternative to ElasticSearch ⚡ 🔍 ✨ Fast, typo tolerant, in-memory fuzzy Search Engine for building delightful search experiences
libraries.pypi_summary: Python client for Typesense, an open source and typo tolerant search engine.
libraries.pro: UX. Typo-tolerance out of the box.
libraries.con: Scale. Not built for massive scientific aggregation.

libraries.name: Milvus
libraries.github_description: Milvus is a high-performance, cloud-native vector database built for scalable vector ANN search
libraries.pypi_summary: Python Sdk for Milvus
libraries.pro: Cloud Native. Good if we move to a cluster later.
libraries.con: Overkill. Too complex for a single PhD server.

libraries.name: Qdrant
libraries.github_description: Qdrant - High-performance, massive-scale Vector Database and Vector Search Engine for the next generation of AI. Also available in the cloud https://cloud.qdrant.io/
libraries.pypi_summary: Client library for the Qdrant vector search engine
libraries.pro: Rust. Fast and safe; good filter support.
libraries.con: Niche. Less documentation than FAISS.

libraries.name: Pinecone
libraries.github_description: The Pinecone Python client
libraries.pypi_summary: Pinecone client and SDK
libraries.pro: Easy. Managed service.
libraries.con: Cost. Expensive for millions of vectors.

libraries.name: DuckDB
libraries.github_description: DuckDB is an analytical in-process SQL database management system
libraries.pypi_summary: DuckDB in-process database
libraries.pro: Local Power. Processes millions of rows on a laptop (Columnar). No server needed.
libraries.con: Concurrency. Only one process can write at a time.

libraries.name: KuzuDB
libraries.github_description: Embedded property graph database built for speed. Vector search and full-text search built in. Implements Cypher.
libraries.pypi_summary: Highly scalable, extremely fast, easy-to-use embeddable graph database
libraries.pro: Simplicity. An embedded Graph DB (like DuckDB but for graphs).
libraries.con: New. Smaller community than Neo4j.

libraries.name: PostgreSQL
libraries.github_description: Open-source vector similarity search for Postgres
libraries.pypi_summary: PostgreSQL database adapter for Python
libraries.pro: Safety. The reliable place to store user data and job status.
libraries.con: Slow Analytics. Bad for querying millions of gene rows.

libraries.name: SBERT
libraries.github_description: State-of-the-Art Text Embeddings
libraries.pypi_summary: Embeddings, Retrieval, and Reranking
libraries.pro: Baseline. Fast, standard embeddings for general similarity.
libraries.con: Lossy. Compresses too much detail for scientific use.

libraries.name: Prefect
libraries.github_description: Prefect is a workflow orchestration framework for building resilient data pipelines in Python.
libraries.pypi_summary: Workflow orchestration and management.
libraries.pro: Modern. Python-native workflow management; handles retries automatically.
libraries.con: Backend. Requires setting up a server for the UI.

libraries.name: Airflow
libraries.github_description: Apache Airflow - A platform to programmatically author, schedule, and monitor workflows
libraries.pypi_summary: Programmatically author, schedule and monitor data pipelines
libraries.pro: Enterprise. The "Big Corp" standard.
libraries.con: Heavy. Overkill for a single researcher.

libraries.name: Dagster
libraries.github_description: An orchestration platform for the development, production, and observation of data assets.
libraries.pypi_summary: Dagster is an orchestration platform for the development, production, and observation of data assets.
libraries.pro: Data. Focuses on the "Asset" rather than the "Task".
libraries.con: Niche. Steeper learning curve.

libraries.name: RabbitMQ
libraries.github_description: Open source RabbitMQ: core server and tier 1 (built-in) plugins
libraries.pro: Decoupling. Buffers jobs so the GPU doesn't crash if 1000 PDFs arrive at once.
libraries.con: Ops. Another service to manage/monitor.

libraries.name: Pandas
libraries.github_description: Flexible and powerful data analysis / manipulation library for Python, providing labeled data structures similar to R data.frame objects, statistical functions, and much more
libraries.pypi_summary: Powerful data structures for data analysis, time series, and statistics
libraries.pro: Universal. Every library speaks Pandas.
libraries.con: Memory. Can blow up RAM on large datasets.

libraries.name: Polars
libraries.github_description: Extremely fast Query Engine for DataFrames, written in Rust
libraries.pypi_summary: Blazingly fast DataFrame library
libraries.pro: Performance. Multi-threaded speed for the heavy data lifting.
libraries.con: API. Syntax is different from Pandas.

libraries.name: NumPy
libraries.github_description: The fundamental package for scientific computing with Python.
libraries.pypi_summary: Fundamental package for array computing in Python
libraries.pro: Foundation. The bedrock of all scientific computing in Python.
libraries.con: Low Level. We rarely touch it directly, but everything depends on it.

libraries.name: Ragas
libraries.github_description: Supercharge Your LLM Application Evaluations 🚀
libraries.pypi_summary: Evaluation framework for RAG and LLM applications
libraries.pro: Methodology. Algorithmically scores "Faithfulness" (Did we hallucinate?).
libraries.con: LLM Dep. Relies on an LLM to do the grading.

libraries.name: PyTorch
libraries.github_description: Tensors and Dynamic neural networks in Python with strong GPU acceleration
libraries.pypi_summary: Tensors and Dynamic neural networks in Python with strong GPU acceleration
libraries.pro: Research. The standard for all modern AI models.
libraries.con: Versions. CUDA version mismatches are a pain.

libraries.name: Transformers
libraries.github_description: 🤗 Transformers: the model-definition framework for state-of-the-art machine learning models in text, vision, audio, and multimodal models, for both inference and training.
libraries.pypi_summary: Transformers: the model-definition framework for state-of-the-art machine learning models in text, vision, audio, and multimodal models, for both inference and training.
libraries.pro: Hub. Access to 100k+ models (HuggingFace).
libraries.con: Bloat. The library is huge and changes often.

libraries.name: scipy.stats
libraries.github_description: SciPy library main repository
libraries.pypi_summary: Fundamental algorithms for scientific computing in Python
libraries.pro: Tests. Standard t-tests and distributions.
libraries.con: CPU. Not accelerated for massive data.

libraries.name: statsmodels
libraries.github_description: Statsmodels: statistical modeling and econometrics in Python
libraries.pypi_summary: Statistical computations and models for Python
libraries.pro: Diagnostics. Deep statistical checks (p-values, R-squared).
libraries.con: Slow. Slower than sklearn.

libraries.name: scikit-learn
libraries.github_description: scikit-learn: machine learning in Python
libraries.pypi_summary: A set of python modules for machine learning and data mining
libraries.pro: Toolbox. Feature selection and basic ML.
libraries.con: Simple. Not for Deep Learning.

libraries.name: Matplotlib
libraries.github_description: matplotlib: plotting with Python
libraries.pypi_summary: Python plotting package
libraries.pro: Publication. Total control over every pixel for the thesis.
libraries.con: Ugly. Default plots look dated.

libraries.name: Seaborn
libraries.github_description: Statistical data visualization in Python
libraries.pypi_summary: Statistical data visualization
libraries.pro: Quick. Beautiful statistical plots instantly.
libraries.con: Limited. Hard to customize deeply.

libraries.name: Rich
libraries.github_description: Rich is a Python library for rich text and beautiful formatting in the terminal.
libraries.pypi_summary: Render rich text, tables, progress bars, syntax highlighting, markdown and more to the terminal
libraries.pro: Monitoring. Live progress bars in the terminal.
libraries.con: Local. Only visible if watching the screen.

libraries.name: crowd-kit
libraries.github_description: Control the quality of your labeled data with the Python tools you already know.
libraries.pypi_summary: Computational Quality Control for Crowdsourcing
libraries.pro: Consensus. Aggregation algorithms for multi-annotator agreement.
libraries.con: Niche. Designed for crowdsourcing, needs adaptation for model voting.

libraries.name: crowd-kit
libraries.github_description: Control the quality of your labeled data with the Python tools you already know.
libraries.pypi_summary: Computational Quality Control for Crowdsourcing
libraries.pro: Consensus. Aggregation algorithms for multi-annotator agreement.
libraries.con: Niche. Designed for crowdsourcing, needs adaptation for model voting.
